name: send
context: Send transactional emails via the Resend API using HTML, plain text, or React Email templates
info: This skill covers sending emails through Resend's REST API and SDKs. It supports three content modes: raw HTML strings, plain text, and React Email components rendered server-side. Agents use this skill to send welcome emails, password resets, order confirmations, invoices, notifications, and any programmatic email. The API accepts recipients (to, cc, bcc), reply-to addresses, custom headers, file attachments, tracking tags, scheduled delivery times, and idempotency keys for deduplication. Both the Node.js SDK (`resend` npm package) and Python SDK (`resend` pip package) wrap the `POST /emails` endpoint with typed interfaces. Batch sending supports up to 100 emails per request via `POST /emails/batch`. See [[resend/auth]] for API key setup, [[resend/domains]] for verified sender domains, and [[resend/react-email]] for building React-based templates.

sections.title: Basic email sending
sections.body: The minimum send requires `from`, `to`, `subject`, and one content field (`html`, `text`, or `react`). The `from` address must be from a verified domain (see [[resend/domains]]). The SDK returns `{ data, error }` — always check `error` before using `data`. The `data.id` is the email ID for tracking.

sections.title: Sending with React templates
sections.body: Pass a React Email component to the `react` parameter instead of `html`. Components must be called as functions: `WelcomeEmail({ name })` not `<WelcomeEmail />`. The SDK renders the component to HTML server-side before sending. This is Node.js SDK only — Python and other SDKs use `html` strings. See [[resend/react-email]] for building templates.

sections.title: Batch sending
sections.body: Send up to 100 emails in one request via `resend.batch.send([...])`. Each item in the array is a full email payload. Returns an array of `{ id }` objects. Useful for sending personalized emails to a list without making individual API calls.

sections.title: File attachments
sections.body: Attach files via the `attachments` array. Each attachment needs `filename` and either `content` (Buffer/base64) or `path` (URL to fetch). Common use cases: invoices (PDF), receipts, reports, and images. Keep total attachment size under 40MB.

sections.title: Scheduled delivery
sections.body: Pass `scheduledAt` as an ISO 8601 datetime string to schedule future delivery. Useful for reminders, drip campaigns, and time-zone-aware notifications. Cancel scheduled emails via `POST /emails/:id/cancel` before the scheduled time.

sections.title: Idempotency and deduplication
sections.body: Pass `idempotencyKey` as a unique string to prevent duplicate sends. If the same key is used within 24 hours, Resend returns the original response without sending again. Use order IDs, event IDs, or UUIDs as keys. Critical for retry-safe integrations.

related.note: A valid API key is required to send emails
The from address must use a verified domain
React Email templates can be passed to the react parameter

scripts.name: Send Email
scripts.description: Send a transactional email via the Resend API using the Node.js SDK

RESEND_API_KEY.description: Resend API key starting with re_

depends.reason: A valid Resend API key is required to send emails
The from address must use a verified domain for production sending
